_comment: News trigger fires only if an article title/summary matches >=1 keyword AND comes from a whitelisted RSS feed below. Matching is case-insensitive substring matching on title+summary.
_comment_google_news: Google News RSS search feeds (news.google.com/rss/search?q=...) -- no API key, no account, publicly documented URL format, used the same way as any other RSS feed below. Added for AI and broader markets/finance coverage the outlet-specific feeds above don't reach on their own -- hence the added AI/finance keywords above, so articles from these topic searches can actually pass the keyword filter. Same 'broken feed logged and skipped, never a hard failure' handling as every other feed (see news_rss.py).
_comment_ai_feeds: Before adding TechCrunch/The Verge/VentureBeat, AI coverage relied only on the generic 'Google News: AI' keyword search -- these three add dedicated AI-outlet feeds (product launches, funding, research) on top of that. Same fail-safe handling: a dead/wrong feed URL is caught per-feed (news_rss.py), logged, and skipped -- never a hard failure for the whole run.
_comment_general_news_feeds: BBC News/The Guardian/Euronews added (2026-07-22) as general-news sources alongside the crypto/finance/AI outlets above -- same URLs already vetted for config/world_news.json's unconditional world-news feed, reused here since they're known-good. Unlike world_news.json, these ARE keyword-gated same as every other feed in this file -- a BBC or Guardian article only triggers a post if it matches config/keywords.json's keyword list.
_comment_pacing: No hard daily stop (2026-07-22) -- posted_count_today crossing throttle_after_daily_posts doesn't block further posting for the rest of the day, it switches the per-run cap down to throttled_max_articles_per_run instead, so a genuinely busy news day keeps a steady trickle through the evening rather than going silent once an arbitrary count is hit. Below the threshold, up to max_articles_per_run articles can go out in a single run if that many genuinely match (see news_alerts.py's inter-article delay for why several in one run still don't land seconds apart). The real safety net against runaway spend is ctx.budget's shared, absolute monthly cap -- these numbers are pacing knobs, not cost controls.
